entries.item: .t-* utility classes do not yet consume the --type-* role tokens (values duplicated in src/index.css)
entries.decision: F1 shipped tokens only to avoid behavioral risk; the .t-* refactor rides the F3 primitive ports where each class is re-verified
entries.upgradeTrigger: F3 net-new-primitives PR opens (the ports consume --type-* and prove the values)

entries.item: --brand-shadow-* call sites not yet migrated to canonical --shadow-*
entries.decision: Two shadow families coexist deliberately; deprecation direction documented in src/tokens.css comment (F1)
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase Z consolidation closeout (per-surface retirement sweep)

entries.item: pr-check 'Non-standard transition duration' rule still allows the legacy 120/180/400ms literal set alongside var(--dur-*) — zero legacy call sites exist repo-wide (verified by adversarial review 2026-07-03), so the allowance + rule message should be retired to make motion tokens the sole standard
entries.decision: Motion tokens ratified as canonical (F2a). The legacy literal allowance is kept only so pre-rebuild code additions stay possible during the transition; this is a policy retirement, NOT a call-site migration (none exist)
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase Z consolidation closeout — remove the legacy ALLOWED set from the rule and update its message so var(--dur-*) is the only standard

entries.item: The kit Toast visuals are not yet ported onto the existing src/components/Toast.tsx — F3 deliberately did NOT build a second Toast (HEAD's ToastProvider/useToast is the canonical mutation-feedback primitive, mounted in App.tsx with 15+ consumers). The kit Toast's look becomes a tokens-only restyle of the existing component (T1 carry-over-then-reskin).
entries.decision: Keep the existing Toast as the single mutation-feedback primitive; restyle its visuals to the kit spec later rather than forking a duplicate during F3 (adversarial review CP1).
entries.upgradeTrigger: Keywords pilot ships mutations (the first rebuilt surface that fires toasts) — restyle then so the new look lands with real usage.

entries.item: RebuiltSidebar footer utilities (NotificationBell, WorkspaceSelector, Revenue/Settings/theme/logout IconButtons) carry over from the legacy Sidebar; a fully DS-native restyle is deferred.
entries.decision: T1 carry-over — machinery-dense, zero capability loss; the visual seam is accepted for F4 so the pilot can validate the registry-driven shell first.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase A admin fan-out reaches the shell chrome.

entries.item: NAV_REGISTRY icons render as lucide via <Icon as>; FA-name mapping of registry entries is deferred.
entries.decision: D5 keeps <Icon as={LucideIcon}> during the incremental lucide-to-FA migration; remapping the registry icons now is out of F4 scope.
entries.upgradeTrigger: The lucide-to-FA call-site migration reaches navRegistry.

entries.item: ~381 files still import lucide-react directly (or use <Icon as={LucideIcon}>). The icon system of record is now Font Awesome Sharp Regular (<Icon name=...>); lucide is kept as a supported migration path but every surface should migrate to FA glyphs.
entries.decision: D5 reversed to Font Awesome Sharp Regular (owner, 2026-07-03). This foundation PR ships the font + Icon component + ICON_NAMES + gate flip; the 381-site migration is explicitly incremental (surface by surface, Keywords pilot first) to keep each PR reviewable rather than one big-bang rewrite.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Per-surface rebuild PRs migrate their lucide icons to <Icon name=...>; retire the lucide `as` path once the last surface is migrated.

entries.item: The self-hosted Font Awesome ships the FULL Pro CSS (fontawesome.min.css ~158KB) + the full Sharp Regular woff2 (~288KB, all glyphs) while the app uses only ~52 ICON_NAMES glyphs.
entries.decision: Ship the complete CSS + font now for correctness/simplicity (any glyph renders); gzip brings the CSS to ~30KB. Subsetting is a perf optimization, not correctness.
entries.upgradeTrigger: The bundle-budget ratchet flags Font Awesome as the active regression source, or the icon migration (DEF-icon-001) settles the final glyph set — then subset the woff2 (pyftsubset) + trim the CSS to the used glyphs.

entries.item: The rebuilt shell has no way to express focus mode — legacy Sidebar takes hidden/onExitHidden and collapses to a slim exit strip (App.tsx effectiveFocusMode, fires on the rewrite surface). RebuiltSidebar/AppShell omit it.
entries.decision: Not a pilot regression — focus mode only fires on Page Rewriter and the pilot is Keywords-only. Ledgered so it is closed before the rebuilt shell expands to cover the Page Rewriter surface (review PR #1478).
entries.upgradeTrigger: The rebuild reaches the Page Rewriter surface (Phase A) — add a hidden/collapsed shell state to AppShell + RebuiltSidebar before the shell replaces the legacy one there.

entries.item: The rebuilt breadcrumb/topbar drops the visible ⌘K search IconButton and the breadcrumb workspace-switcher dropdown that legacy Breadcrumbs render. The ⌘K keyboard shortcut still works (global listener) and workspace switching is covered by the sidebar selector — this is a secondary/discoverability affordance loss, not a capability loss.
entries.decision: Accepted for F4 (shortcut + sidebar switcher preserve the underlying capability). Re-add the visible top-bar search affordance before the rebuilt shell replaces the legacy one platform-wide (review PR #1478).
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase A shell-expansion, or the first mouse-user report of the missing search button — add a ⌘K IconButton to the rebuilt Toolbar/breadcrumb.

entries.item: Inline list-row rank sparkline and 7-day delta pill from the mockup are deferred; the drawer sparkline remains because it uses the existing per-keyword history endpoint.
entries.decision: Avoid an N+1 history fetch in the pilot table. Ship the DS-native row without inline trend graphics until a batched history-by-keyword read model exists.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Add a batched rank-history read model for visible keyword rows, then render the row sparkline/delta through the DataTable cell without client-side data fabrication.

entries.item: Avg-position and period-over-period delta KPI variants are deferred; the pilot renders existing count and traffic-value tiles only.
entries.decision: Do not compute new summary rollups or deltas in the client. The KPI variants need a server-owned rollup/snapshot-diff contract before they can be trusted.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Server summary exposes average-position and comparison-period deltas with tests that define the date window and empty-state semantics.

entries.item: Retiring lint:ds-adherence (D-F2b-1) drops the only check that flagged raw-px literals in inline-style DS components. The F2a ds-* gates cover hex/palette/motion/icon purity but NOT raw pixel values, so raw-px in inline-style primitives is now unenforced.
entries.decision: The kit adherence config was redundant with the F2a ds-* gates plus TypeScript prop-checking for everything except raw-px. Rather than keep a whole lint pass alive for one signal, the raw-px gap is accepted now and re-addressed as a focused rule when inline-style primitives settle.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase Z consolidation closeout (or when inline-style DS primitives stabilize) — add a focused raw-px pr-check rule, or migrate inline px to the --space-* scale.

entries.item: RebuiltAppChrome/AppShell has no slot for the connection/health StatusBar (hasOpenAIKey/hasWebflowToken/connected/workspaceCount) the legacy shell renders — AppShell exposes sidebar/topbar/children but no footer. Review PR #1480 restored CommandPalette (⌘K) + AdminChat to the rebuilt mount (real capability losses), but StatusBar needs a shell footer slot.
entries.decision: Ship the rebuilt Keywords surface without the StatusBar footer for now — the RebuiltSidebar footer's NotificationBell already surfaces connection state, and adding an AppShell footer slot is a shell-primitive enhancement, not a Keywords-pilot bug. The two capability-loss items (⌘K, admin chat) were fixed in-PR; this display-affordance is ledgered.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Phase A shell-expansion (the rebuilt shell covers non-Keywords surfaces) — add a `footer` slot to AppShell + thread StatusBar through RebuiltAppChrome so every rebuilt surface shows connection/health state.

entries.item: The Pages/Clusters/Lifecycle grouped lenses group only the CURRENT PAGE of rows (pageSize <= 100), not the full workspace keyword set — so BoardColumn counts and group stats reflect page 1 while the lens tab shows the true total. Review PR #1480 made this HONEST (a truncation banner + removed the fabricated client-side averageRank) but full coverage needs server-side grouping.
entries.decision: Ship the grouped lenses over the current page WITH an explicit truncation banner (never present a page-1 board as the full universe) rather than fetch-all-client-side (unbounded) or block the pilot on a server grouping contract. averageRank removed per DEF-kw-002 (no client rollups).
entries.upgradeTrigger: A server-side grouped-view read model (group by page/cluster/lifecycleStage across ALL keywords with server-computed counts + rollups) — then the grouped lenses render complete groups without the page cap.

entries.item: hubSortToKccSort was extracted to the shared src/lib/keywordHubSort.ts (the rebuilt surface consumes it), but the legacy KeywordHub.tsx still keeps its own byte-identical copy + HubSortKey type. Two mappings can drift.
entries.decision: Do NOT touch the legacy KeywordHub during the pilot (a deliberate Task 8.4 constraint — it is the surface being replaced). The drift window is transient: KeywordHub is retired when ui-rebuild-shell covers Keywords, and the duplicate dies with it. Unifying now risks destabilizing legacy for no lasting benefit.
entries.upgradeTrigger: KeywordHub retirement (the rebuilt Keywords surface goes global / legacy branch removed) — delete the legacy hubSortToKccSort + HubSortKey and import from src/lib/keywordHubSort.

entries.item: The rebuilt surface fetches summary+rows as ONE initial-view query keyed on the full rowsQuery, so the summary (counts + topicClusters + cannibalization + computeOrganicTrafficValue) rebuilds on every sort/page/lens/filter change though it is invariant to sort/page; computeOrganicTrafficValue also re-SELECT*s page_keywords despite the lite sourceSnapshot already being in memory. A standalone summary hook keyed WITHOUT query already exists.
entries.decision: Efficiency, not correctness — the surface is right, just does redundant work per interaction. Deferred rather than rushed: decoupling the summary from rowsQuery + threading the snapshot into computeOrganicTrafficValueFromPages needs careful type alignment (lite vs full PageKeywordMap) and is a template pattern worth getting right, not fast.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Before Phase A fan-out leans on the initial-view pattern — split the summary into its own query keyed without sort/page (reuse the existing summary hook), and feed computeOrganicTrafficValueFromPages the in-memory sourceSnapshot.pageMap instead of re-reading page_keywords.

entries.item: Save-draft / publish spine — full-page Webflow write-back (SB-032) is not built; the rebuilt surface is export-only v1 (.docx/.pdf + push-to-draft-target).
entries.decision: AD-017 export-only parity v1; any new Webflow write path is a flagged, owner-signed follow-up. Rewrites export out rather than writing back to the CMS.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Owner signs off the seo-editor-write-targets contract + a dark-launch flag for CMS write-back.

entries.item: Rewrite-playbook writes (SB-031) deferred; v1 renders the playbook patterns as static read-only chips.
entries.decision: Playbook read may already be serialized (admin-workspace-view); write/edit of playbook patterns is out of export-only v1 scope.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Playbook editing prioritized on the roadmap.

entries.item: Server-persisted rewrite drafts (SB-030) deferred; v1 keeps drafts in localStorage (the existing quick-win).
entries.decision: localStorage draft is the v1 persistence; server-persisted drafts are a later tier.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Cross-device draft persistence requested, or the write spine (DEF-page-rewriter-001) lands.

entries.item: Competitor→insight graduation write is not built; the rebuilt surface renders existing insightId linkage only, it does not create/graduate insights.
entries.decision: AD-004 defers all 'graduate to Insights Engine' writes wholesale to the one C3-era owner-signed cross-surface contract; no surface builds an ad-hoc graduation write.
entries.upgradeTrigger: The C3 cross-surface insight-graduation contract lands.

entries.item: 49-point geo-grid rank map is not built; the surface ships a posture grid on real per-market single-point data instead of a geo-grid scan.
entries.decision: AD-025: no data source exists and the geo-grid needs a new paid scan job + snapshot table + migration; ship the posture grid now and defer the scan.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Owner greenlights the paid geo-grid scan job (SB-057 / sn-local-presence-1).

entries.item: View-on-Google link is deferred; no review/maps URL is persisted so the surface cannot deep-link a reviewer to the live Google review.
entries.decision: SB-018-5 needs a DB-column + mapper + serialization lockstep to persist the maps URL; defer that column and ship review-response authoring without the outbound link.
entries.upgradeTrigger: SB-018-5 lands the maps-URL column + mapper + serialization (sn-local-presence-5).

entries.item: Reopen-for-edits on an approved review response is deferred; the approved→draft transition is illegal in the state machine today.
entries.decision: SB-018-7 needs state-machine sign-off (AD-024) to add the reopen transitions that clear approval metadata; ship the forward lifecycle now and defer reopen.
entries.upgradeTrigger: State-machine sign-off adds the approved→draft reopen transitions (SB-018-7 / sn-local-presence-7).

entries.item: Per-target nudge-client rate-limit is deferred; the existing remindDeliverable path has no per-target throttle so repeat nudges are not rate-limited on this surface.
entries.decision: SB-014's email/broadcast/activity halves already exist; only the net-new per-target throttle is missing and it did not ride W2, so ledger it rather than ship an ad-hoc throttle.
entries.upgradeTrigger: SB-014 per-target rate-limit ships (sn-local-presence-4 / N7).

entries.item: GBP Performance hero metrics (views/calls/directions) are cut; the surface shows rating + reviews + completeness instead of live performance metrics.
entries.decision: AD-025: SB-058 needs a new Google Performance OAuth scope + client method + storage that the GBP client does not have today; ship the available signals and defer the performance ingestion.
entries.upgradeTrigger: Owner greenlights the new Google Performance scope + ingestion (SB-058 / sn-local-presence-2).

entries.item: Profile-health checklist ships 3 real signals (photos/attributes/category); hours/products/Q&A/service-area signals are deferred.
entries.decision: Only photos/attributes/category are captured by GBP ingestion today; ship the 3-signal checklist and defer the extended-profile signals until ingestion covers them.
entries.upgradeTrigger: SB-056 extended GBP profile ingestion lands (hours/products/Q&A/service-area) (sn-local-presence-8).
